content.claim: A documented Finnish study explicitly examines the intersection between pupils' support-tier level and school intake/enrolment under choice-based systems ('The dilemma of inclusion and school choice — pupils' support-tier level and lower-secondary schools' intake in Turku', Lempinen, Berisha, & Seppänen 2016), indicating that SEN status may interact with school choice outcomes.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Finnish system reform lens
limitations: Citation retrieved; full-text findings not available in research notes and would require separate retrieval
Study limited to single municipality (Turku) and specific time period (lower-secondary intake)
Whether support-tier assignment affects choice-system access or merely correlates with family choice patterns not specified in available source extract
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
